assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item in the consolidated financial statements.
The question concerns hedge-accounting model identification for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised receivable is a recognised asset/liability type of hedged item under IFRS 9, making the fair value hedge model a relevant top-level hedge-accounting approach.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies highly probable forecast transactions as hedged items, so the cash flow hedge model is a peer canonical hedge-accounting model relevant to foreign-currency hedging analysis.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 establish a distinct hedge-accounting model for foreign-exchange exposures that form part of a net investment in a foreign operation.
